name: Craft CMS GraphQL and REST API
what_it_does: Flexible PHP content management system with GraphQL and REST API — enabling developers to create custom content structures with the content modeling engine, build traditional or headless websites, and integrate with frontend frameworks (Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby) or native Twig templates, with Craft Commerce for e-commerce and a powerful plugin ecosystem.
use_cases: Querying Craft CMS content entries via GraphQL API for headless frontend (Next.js, Nuxt) integration
Creating and updating content entries via Craft CMS REST API for content automation and migration
Managing Craft CMS assets, categories, and tags via API for content pipeline integration
Building headless e-commerce with Craft Commerce via GraphQL API
Integrating Craft CMS content with external systems via element API plugin for custom REST endpoints
not_for: Non-developer-managed content (WordPress is more approachable for non-technical editors)
Enterprise headless CMS with brand governance (use Contentful or Storyblok for enterprise headless)
Rapid CMS setup without developer (Craft requires PHP developer for setup and templates)
best_when: A development agency or custom website team needs maximum content modeling flexibility with a mature PHP CMS — especially for complex information architecture that doesn't fit WordPress's post/page model.
avoid_when: You need a managed cloud headless CMS without server management, have non-technical editors requiring drag-and-drop page building, or need enterprise SaaS with SLAs.

auth.notes: API key for GraphQL and element API access (schema-based access controls). OAuth2 via first-party plugin for user-delegated access. GraphQL schema with public and private token scopes (read-only vs. mutation access). Webhook for element save and delete events. Native GraphQL API built into Craft 3.3+. Element API plugin for custom REST endpoints. User-authenticated sessions for full CMS access. Server-side deployment required (PHP + MySQL/PostgreSQL).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Solo: free forever for single developer; limited features
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $299/project one-time; Enterprise custom; Craft Commerce $999/project one-time
pricing.notes: Pittsburgh company (Pixel & Tonic). Founded 2012. Bootstrapped and profitable. Craft is self-hosted (PHP + MySQL/PostgreSQL) — no SaaS hosting included. Craft Cloud hosted option launched 2023. One-time perpetual license for Pro (not subscription) is key differentiator. 175,000+ active installs. Strong agency adoption. Used by Netflix, Square, Tesla, Pfizer. Craft Commerce for e-commerce. Plugin ecosystem with 800+ plugins. Twig templating for traditional CMS. GraphQL for headless/API. PHP 8+ required. YiiFramework-based architecture.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — rate limits controlled by deployment infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API built-in for headless use. REST API via Element API plugin. API key auth with schema-based scopes. Webhook for content events. Self-hosted means no rate limits beyond deployment infrastructure. Excellent developer documentation. PHP developer required for setup and customization. Twig templates for traditional rendering; GraphQL for headless. One-time license model unique in CMS market.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL queries fully idempotent. Entry reads idempotent. Entry mutations require unique slug for idempotency. Asset reads idempotent. Category reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted — requires PHP + MySQL/PostgreSQL server management unless using Craft Cloud
GraphQL schema must be configured per content model — not automatic out of the box
GraphQL mutations require Pro license (not Solo/free)
Element API plugin required for custom REST endpoints — separate from native GraphQL
PHP developer required for deployment, plugin development, and template customization

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (controlled by deployment). GDPR compliant (controller responsibility with self-hosting). Self-hosted — data residency controlled by operator. API key with schema-based scopes. Self-hosted means full control over security posture. GraphQL introspection should be disabled in production. API key tokens must be rotated regularly. CSRF protection built in. Session-based auth for CMS admin. Plugin ecosystem security varies — audit plugins before production use.
